excerpt: What does it mean for a predictor to be calibrated, how far can it stray from perfect calibration, and why does a cheap online algorithm plus a single weak learner get you most of the way to multicalibration? A tour via La Masia.
Five definitions of the matrix trace (from eigenvalue sums to Jordan forms to Dirac notation), and its role in quantum mechanics via density matrices, the Born rule, and partial trace.
An introduction to q-ary lattices and their central role in lattice-based cryptography, including the SIS problem, worst-case to average-case reductions, and collision-resistant hash functions.
A discussion on which search problems are not self-reducible, the complexity landscape of TFNP, and the subclasses PLS and PPAD; with a focus on downward self-reducibility and open questions.
An introduction to self-reducibility and search-to-decision reductions, with worked examples for SAT and CLIQUE, downward self-reducibility, Mahaney's theorem, and the TFNP landscape.
An introduction to the mathematical technique of reduction (Karp, Truth Table, and Cook reductions) and their role in computability and complexity theory.
